mnemonics: BUBBLE BLOWS UP, SELECTION PICKS ONE: Bubble Sort repeatedly 'blows' the largest element to the end like a bubble rising. Selection Sort 'selects' the smallest element and places it at the front.
SELECT THE BEST, BUBBLE THE REST: Selection finds the minimum element (the 'best') first. Bubble compares neighbors and swaps the 'rest' until sorted.
keyPoints: Selection Sort scans the unsorted part to find the minimum element and swaps it to the sorted front. Time Complexity: O(n²) in all cases.

Bubble Sort repeatedly compares and swaps adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order, 'bubbling' the largest value to the end each pass. Time Complexity: O(n²) average/worst, O(n) best case (if nearly sorted).
Both are comparison-based, in-place sorting algorithms but are inefficient for large datasets due to quadratic time complexity.
commonMistakes: Confusing the algorithms: Using Selection Sort's 'select and swap at the end' logic when trying to implement Bubble Sort, or vice versa.
Off-by-one errors: Incorrect loop boundaries (e.g., going one index too far) leading to array index out of bounds exceptions or missing the last element.
